Actually spec says Return JSON only with this exact shape: {"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","prompt_version":"v1","model_slug":"<provider model id>","provider":"<provider slug>","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{...},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}.

So we need to set "kind":"tool_call". Provide prompt_version "v1". Provide model_slug maybe placeholder like "gpt-4". But must be provider model id? The instruction says <provider model id>. Could be something like "provider_1". But maybe we can use "gpt-4". Provide provider slug maybe "openai". Use quotes.

assistant_message: brief status, maybe "Searching for fixture loading code". Keep short.

tool_call: tool_name "search", payload with pattern and path.

acquired_fact_ids: array with one fact id, maybe "fct_000000000001". Provide that.

readiness_state: maybe "evidence_visible" because we have evidence visible after search? Or "evidence_acquired"? Since we haven't actually acquired yet, but we are about to call tool, maybe readiness_state "evidence_visible". Choose "evidence_visible". Provide that.

Thus final JSON.

Make sure no extra fields. No markdown. Provide only JSON.

Let's craft:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "prompt_version":"v1",
  "model_slug":"gpt-4",
  "provider":"openai",
  "assistant_message":"Searching for fixture loading references",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"search",
    "payload":{
      "pattern":"fixture",
      "path":"crates/graphbench-core/src"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":["fct_000000000001"],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}
